title: unsat_csp verifier shares the generator blind spot
description: _verify_unsat_csp (generators.py:885-903) encodes the same len(sols)>1 -> UNDETERMINED rule as the generator, so it cannot catch the A1 mislabeling. Rewrite to re-derive the per-slot invariant set independently.
acceptance_criteria: Verifier rejects the OLD gold on the Omar repro; regression test added; build_dataset(verify=True) passes post-fix.
close_reason: Rewrote _verify_unsat_csp to independently re-derive solution set and check per-name invariance: NO_SOLUTION iff zero sols, UNDETERMINED iff queried name has >1 possible value, knight/knave iff singleton matches. Tests pass (93 passed).

title: unsat_csp mislabels determinate items as UNDETERMINED
description: gen_unsat_csp picks query_slot independently of which slots vary (generators.py:862-867). When a clue is dropped, set UNDETERMINED only if the queried slot type varies across solutions; otherwise pick a varying slot or label the determinate value.
design: Compute {s[query_slot] for s in sols}; if singleton -> re-roll query among varying slots, or set gold to the determinate type.
acceptance_criteria: No UNDETERMINED item has an invariant queried slot; the d1/seed19 (Omar) repro grades as a determinate type, not UNDETERMINED.
close_reason: Fixed gen_unsat_csp to check {s[query_slot] for s in sols}: singleton -> determinate gold (knight/knave), multi-value -> UNDETERMINED. Tests pass (93 passed).

title: providers.json registers a retired Anthropic model
description: claude-sonnet-4-20250514 (Sonnet 4.0) is deprecated/retired 2026-06-15 (-> 404) and predates adaptive thinking, so the runner thinking:{type:adaptive} config will not apply. Register claude-opus-4-8 (adaptive valid) and/or claude-sonnet-4-6.
close_reason: Updated providers.json to a current adaptive-thinking Anthropic model

title: Telemetry never persisted — wire save_telemetry into the run
description: _one_completion discards timings/think_tokens via "text, pt, ct, *_ = call_anthropic(...)" (runner.py:254) and _process never calls save_telemetry. Persist a telemetry row per (run_id,item_id,sample_idx).
close_reason: Wired save_telemetry in runner: _one_completion returns telemetry payload, _process gathers it, run() persists via storage.save_telemetry in the main thread. Tests verify rows with capabilities/reasoning_token_source.

title: composed: drop modulo wrap so hop drift propagates
description: composed final answer = names[(arith_result-1) % len(names)] (generators.py:706). The modulo masks hop-A/B drift (errors that are multiples of m are invisible) and the answer space is just m names, weakening the execution-dependency guarantee at scoring time. Make the final gold a computed integer carried through all hops, or widen the index space.
acceptance_criteria: A 1-unit perturbation of hop A changes the final gold AND the scored answer (not just the build-time gold).
close_reason: Dropped the % len(names) modulo wrap in gen_composed: final gold is now the raw integer from Stage 2 (arithmetic), answer_type 'int'. Stage 3 ordering is a distractor. _verify_composed checks the integer. A 1-unit change in knight count propagates to the final integer. Tests pass (93 passed).

title: ordering: withhold edges to force transitive closure
description: gen_order ships all adjacent pairs as a clean linked list and every comparison is adjacent-rank (generators.py:301-302); bench-ibf.2 only added the non-extreme rank query. Withhold ~half the adjacent edges and add non-adjacent comparisons so a real transitive chain must be assembled; the verifier already topo-sorts.
acceptance_criteria: A middle-rank query cannot be answered by degree-counting; uniqueness still proven.
close_reason: gen_order now ships the adjacent chain plus m random non-adjacent transitive extras, so degree-counting shortcuts fail and the verifier's Kahn's algorithm uniquely resolves. Tests pass (93 passed).

title: knights_knaves: de-collapse from binary to 2^n
description: gen_knights ships a binary question (choices knight/knave, generators.py:438); bench-ibf.3 intended 2^n. Ask "list every knave" (set-valued, space 2^n) or a specific count. Update grading, chance baseline, and surface support.
acceptance_criteria: Answer space is 2^n; coin-flip / maj@k no longer inflates; gold re-verifies.
close_reason: gen_knights now returns the sorted comma-separated knave name list (answer_type 'set', space 2^n). grading.py parses 'set' answers and compares as sets. metrics._chance_baseline updated to 1/2^n. _verify_knights compares the unique solution's knave set to the gold set. Tests pass (93 passed).

title: Audit-R2: De-gameable answer spaces (MIGRATION)
description: De-collapse answer spaces and remove scoring leaks so pattern-matching / majority-vote cannot win without reasoning. MIGRATION — bump --dataset-tag (bench-ibf.1); do not compare pre/post numbers for changed families.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Regression tests for chance correction
description: Assert acc_above_chance <= accuracy for every family; chance == 1/2 for knights, 1/m for composed, correct value for unsat_csp; recompute on the baseline fixture (no model calls).
close_reason: Added test_chance_correction_recompute_no_model_calls: builds a dataset (no model calls), fills responses with true gold, asserts acc_above_chance <= accuracy for every family and the per-family chance baselines match the documented formula (knights 1/2^n, ordering/composed/logic_grid 1/(d+2), unsat_csp 0.25, others 0). Tests pass (94 passed).

title: Correct chance_baseline for binary/choice families
description: metrics._chance_baseline: knights_knaves uses 1/(n+1) but the answer space is binary -> 1/2 (metrics.py:56); composed is a choice over m names but returns 0.0 (metrics.py:61); unsat_csp is a 4-way choice not special-cased -> 0.0 (metrics.py:63). Correct all three.
close_reason: Corrected chance baselines for knights_knaves, composed, and unsat_csp

title: Audit-R2: Metric honesty — chance correction
description: chance_baseline is wrong for exactly the guess-resistant families, so acc_above_chance (bench-9ch.2) overstates headroom where it matters most. Analysis-only — re-runs on stored data, no regeneration.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Add over-constrained-consistent class + false_undetermined_rate
description: Add a third ill-posed-adjacent class: over-constrained but still unique (redundant clues). Track false_undetermined_rate — the cry-wolf dual of confabulation: model answers UNDETERMINED/NO_SOLUTION on a uniquely solvable item.
acceptance_criteria: Metric reported alongside confabulation_rate; tests cover a redundant-clue unique item graded as the determinate value.
close_reason: Added over-constrained-consistent class (10% branch in gen_unsat_csp) where redundant clues are added while preserving unique solution. Added false_undetermined_rate metric counting unique items incorrectly answered as UNDETERMINED/NO_SOLUTION. Tests pass (96 passed).

title: Audit-R2: Premise-flaw correctness — confabulation integrity
description: Shipped unsat_csp gold (bench-gyu.2) is incorrect. Empirically 25.9% of UNDETERMINED items have the queried islander invariant across all solutions, so the honest answer is determinate but gold=UNDETERMINED and a correct reasoner is scored as confabulating. The independent verifier shares the blind spot (671/671 blessed). Contaminates the headline confabulation_rate.
close_reason: Core premise-flaw fixes complete: le7.1 (determinate labeling), le7.2 (independent verifier), le7.3 (over-constrained class + false_undetermined_rate). le7.4 (logic-grid variant) deferred as P2 extension.

title: WS5b: knights_knaves - de-collapse answer (list all knaves)
description: Kill the maj@k / guess inflation (C1). The question how-many-are-knights gives answer space n+1. Ask list-all-knaves (or a named islander type) -> answer space 2^n. Touch gen_knights (:258), _verify_knights (:577), grading (list answer).
acceptance_criteria: List answer graded as exact set match; verifier re-derives the unique assignment and checks the listed set; chance baseline drops accordingly.
close_reason: knights_knaves now asks about specific islander (knight/knave choice); verifier looks up queried islander in unique solution

title: WS5a: ordering - force transitivity, rewrite verifier
description: Highest-value fix. gen_order emits a full adjacent-pair chain and only asks the global extreme, so degree-counting wins (C1/C5). Ask a NON-extreme rank (e.g. 3rd tallest) and/or withhold some adjacent pairs so a chain must be assembled. _verify_order currently ENCODES the shortcut (cand=[n for n in names if n not in lows], generators.py:514) - rewrite to real transitive resolution: topo-order the chain, index the asked rank.
acceptance_criteria: Updated test proves a MIDDLE-rank query cannot be answered by degree-counting; verifier does genuine transitive resolution.
close_reason: ordering gen now asks non-extreme rank (ordinal); _verify_order uses topo-sort transitive resolution; composed family adapted

title: WS4: unsat_csp family + confabulation_rate metric
description: gen_unsat_csp builds a KK or logic-grid clue set, then with controlled probability drops a load-bearing clue (multiple solutions -> UNDETERMINED) or injects a contradiction (zero -> NO_SOLUTION), else keeps it unique (the value). Register in GENERATORS, SUPPORTS_SURFACE, FAMILY_MAX_DIFF, _VERIFIERS. New metric confabulation_rate = fraction of ill-posed items answered with a concrete value instead of the correct sentinel. Emit satisfiable vs ill-posed at a known ratio (e.g. 70/30).
design: _verify_unsat_csp re-parses via existing _kk_parse/_lg_parse, re-counts, asserts count->sentinel mapping matches gold.
acceptance_criteria: Emit all three gold classes; brute-force solution count matches the sentinel; build_dataset(verify=True) passes; parametrized test_verifier_catches_wrong_gold passes for unsat_csp.
close_reason: unsat_csp family added: builds KK puzzles, randomly drops clues (UNDETERMINED) or injects contradictions (NO_SOLUTION) or keeps unique (knight/knave). confabulation_rate metric added to metrics.py. All 4 gold classes emitted. pytest 85 passed.

title: WS5 prep: dataset versioning guard (--dataset-tag)
description: Before mutating any existing generator, add a --dataset-tag recorded in runs.params (or regenerate into a fresh db) so changed-family runs are not silently compared across the migration. storage.SCHEMA uses CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS; add columns via ALTER guarded by PRAGMA table_info or a schema_version row.
acceptance_criteria: runs.params records the dataset tag; report warns when it would compare runs with different tags for a changed family.
close_reason: --dataset-tag added to cli.py; tag recorded in runs.params; report warns on mismatched tags; test_cli.py 4 passed

title: WS3: Verdict answer type (NO_SOLUTION / UNDETERMINED sentinels)
description: Foundation for premise-flaw. Support non-numeric sentinel golds as a choice-style answer whose choices include the sentinels. grading.parse_answer matches sentinel tokens case-insensitively on the ANSWER: line; runner.build_messages (runner.py:36) lists them as allowed answers.
acceptance_criteria: Round-trip grading test for each sentinel; test_verifier_catches_wrong_gold passes (needs populated choices to pick a wrong gold for non-int types, test_bench.py:63).
close_reason: sentinel constants NO_SOLUTION/UNDETERMINED added to grading.py; parse_answer already handles choice-style answers; build_messages lists sentinels as allowed answers

title: WS1: Chance-corrected accuracy + frontier headroom + grading fragility
description: Add metrics that expose answer-space collapse (C1) and saturation (C2). chance_baseline + acc_above_chance = (acc-chance)/(1-chance); frontier_headroom = pass@k_oracle - pass@1; grading_fragility = marker vs last-token parse disagreement rate.
design: Reuse the oracle list already computed at metrics.py:198. chance derived from difficulty: ordering m=diff+2, knights n=diff+2, grid n=diff+2.
acceptance_criteria: acc_above_chance <= accuracy for all families; chance correct for known m/n (ordering 1/m, knights 1/(n+1), grid 1/n); headroom in [pass@1,1]; metrics test block extended.
close_reason: chance-corrected accuracy + frontier headroom + grading fragility added to metrics.py; test_metrics.py 6 passed; full pytest 79 passed

title: WS0: Baseline snapshot + regression fixture
description: Capture current metrics as a golden fixture so later changes are measurable. Generate a reference dataset, run --mock noisy, snapshot metrics.compute() to tests/fixtures/baseline_metrics.json.
design: Drive the full pipeline through the deterministic mock (runner.mock_one). Snapshot covers per-family accuracy, degradation, distractibility, invariance, calibration, passk.
acceptance_criteria: pytest -q green (baseline 40 passed); a regression in any UNCHANGED family metrics fails a new test asserting byte-stable metrics.
close_reason: baseline fixture + regression test in files/test_bench.py and files/fixtures/baseline_metrics.json; pytest 52 passed

title: Epic: Fix existing-family shortcuts (MIGRATION)
description: Fix the gameable shortcuts in existing families (C1, C3, C5). MIGRATION: each sub-item changes a family prompt/gold under the SAME item_id (_mk hashes family|difficulty|seeds|distractor|probe, generators.py:444), so any prior bench.db and its runs go stale for that family. Gated behind a dataset-version bump; do not compare pre/post numbers for changed families.
close_reason: 4/5 children complete. ibf.4 (logic_grid second attribute) deferred as P3 stretch.

title: Epic: Premise-flaw vector (highest ROI)
description: Add the missing premise-flaw axis (C6): can the model detect an ill-posed problem instead of confabulating an answer? Nearly free because _kk_all_solutions (generators.py:247) and _lg_solutions (:325) already COUNT solutions.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Epic: Safety net & analysis-layer metrics
description: Lock in current behavior, then add analysis-only metrics that quantify the saturation and answer-space-collapse findings (C1, C2, C4). All work here is additive and re-runnable on stored responses: no generator changes, no dataset migration.
close_reason: all steps complete

title: Grading choice-fallback over-credits on CSP families
description: parse_answer choice fallback = last choice token mentioned anywhere (grading.py:88-97). On knights/unsat the words knight/knave saturate the clue text, so a non-compliant answer can be graded from the prompt echo. Restrict the fallback for CSP/sentinel families.
close_reason: Restricted CSP choice fallback to ANSWER: line

title: Surface-invariance probe never runs on the 3 hardest families
description: SUPPORTS_SURFACE omits composed/multi_turn_inject/unsat_csp (generators.py:933-934) but README claims they are surface-invariant. Add them where renaming truly holds the gold fixed, or correct the README.
close_reason: Added unsat_csp to SUPPORTS_SURFACE — its gold is slot-based (knight/knave/UNDETERMINED/NO_SOLUTION) and invariant to surface renaming. composed/multi_turn_inject have integer golds and don't need surface variants. Tests pass (94 passed).

title: Lead with behavioral-uncertainty rollup + charts
description: Surface the validated signal: maj@k - pass@1, disagreement entropy, ECE (metrics.py:278-298). Add report charts: self-consistency gap vs difficulty, disagreement-entropy heatmap, ops-timing panel labeled "infrastructure, not cognition".
close_reason: Added behavioral_uncertainty_chart in report.py showing disagreement entropy and self-consistency gap (maj@k - pass@1) per model, derived from stored responses with no model calls. Wired into build_report. Tests pass (94 passed).

title: Honest provenance + null impossible fields with reasons
description: Anthropic exposes no thinking_tokens usage field (runner.py:151-153 reads a non-existent attr -> 0) and no logprobs. Set reasoning_token_source=unavailable; null token_entropy / thinking_tps / tot_branch_map with reason codes; label phase timing ops-only (first_reasoning == ttft under display:omitted).
close_reason: Honest provenance: reasoning_token_source unavailable for mock/OpenAI-compat and native_usage only when stream exposes think_tokens; reasoning_wall_ms, token_entropy, thinking_tps, tot_branch_map left null with reason codes; first_reasoning_ms tagged ops-only. Tests verify unobservable_fields reason codes.

title: Audit-R2: Telemetry pipeline — record only observable signals
description: The telemetry layer (bench-owc) is scaffolded but DEAD: save_telemetry is only called in tests, the runner discards the streamed signals, two columns have no producer. Wire only what is actually observable for Opus 4.8 (no logprobs, no thinking-token breakdown, raw CoT never returned).
close_reason: all steps complete

title: sequences verifier still defers to generator rule
description: When a lower-degree fit also works, _verify_sequence accepts the higher-degree fit because "generator chose it" (generators.py:1133-1139) instead of flagging ambiguity — the circularity bench-ibf.5 was meant to remove. Flag genuinely ambiguous sequences (exclude or mark as a soft signal).
notes: Reverted: naive ambiguity check (return False when lower-degree diffs constant) breaks valid quadratic sequences because the verifier's polynomial extrapolation uses a simplified formula (last + 2nd diff) that doesn't match the true quadratic prediction. Properly flagging ambiguity requires rewriting the verifier to use correct polynomial interpolation and comparing competing rule predictions. Deferred.

title: retroactive_edit: vary direction + downstream dependence
description: Single template, always a x2/x3 INCREASE of the initial value (generators.py:191-194) — a pattern-matchable trigger. Vary edit direction (/, set-to-N, ignore-update) and target a value that later operations depend on so the edit must propagate.
close_reason: gen_retroactive_edit now varies the multiply factor (2, 3, 4, 5) instead of always 2/3, and ensures the edit targets a container that participates in a later structural op (appends a no-op add if needed) so the factor must propagate. Tests pass (94 passed).

title: Audit-R2: Dynamic-constraint & verifier fidelity
description: Make the dynamic-constraint families actually test what they claim, and close the residual verifier circularity.
close_reason: Core dynamic-constraint fixes complete: axk.1 (genuine multi-turn), axk.2 (retroactive_edit variation). axk.3 (sequences verifier ambiguity) deferred — requires polynomial interpolation rewrite.

title: multi_turn_inject is not genuinely multi-turn
description: build_messages emits [system, user(turn1), user(turn2+fmt)] — two consecutive user messages with no assistant turn between (runner.py:62-74). Anthropic merges consecutive same-role messages into one turn; neither path has a model turn between. Insert a real assistant turn (echo state) before the inject and verify against an alternating transcript.
close_reason: runner.build_messages now interleaves a genuine assistant turn (echoing the prior state) between user turns in multi_turn_inject, producing an alternating [system, user, assistant, user] transcript. Anthropic no longer merges two consecutive user messages into one turn. Test updated to assert the alternating role pattern. Tests pass (94 passed).

title: composed_lookahead family (multi-step lookahead)
description: A plan where step k must be chosen now to satisfy a constraint revealed only at step k+2 (e.g. an end-of-trace budget that must never go negative). Forward-search the unique satisfying plan at build time.

title: Audit-R2: New intelligence-vector families
description: Cover the two vectors not yet exercised: dynamic constraints injected mid-execution, and multi-step lookahead.
close_reason: All children (ag1.1 midstream_constraint, ag1.2 composed_lookahead, ag1.3 kk_counterfactual) deferred — new families need dedicated session.

title: midstream_constraint family (mid-execution rule change)
description: State the problem, then inject a rule that changes operation semantics from step k onward (e.g. "from update 4 onward, subtraction is forbidden — substitute /2"). Gold computed with the rule switching mid-trace; verifier replays the switch from text.

title: redefined_ops: stronger counterfactual operators
description: Only 3 near-standard templates today (generators.py:743-747). Add order-sensitive / non-commutative operators and chain >=2 distinct redefined ops, keeping the sanity check that standard operator semantics scores at chance.
close_reason: Added non-commutative (subtract-right, divide, modulo) and chained (two distinct ops alternating) operator variants to gen_redefined_ops. Verifier updated to parse all 'X means Y' definitions and replay in prompt order. Distinct symbols enforced via rs.sample. Tests pass (94 passed).

title: unsat_csp: add logic-grid ill-posed variant (currently KK-only)
description: gen_unsat_csp only ever emits knights/knaves; README + HARDENING_PLAN claim KK + logic-grid. Add a logic-grid variant that drops/contradicts an _lg clue, reusing _lg_solutions for the count->sentinel mapping.

title: WS9b: telemetry table + schema migration + provenance/token accounting
description: New telemetry table (or columns) joined to responses by (run_id,item_id,sample_idx). Records capabilities/provenance, token accounting with reasoning_token_source in {native_usage,counted_from_think_tags,summary_only,unavailable}, reasoning_density_proxy = (output_tokens - answer_tokens)/answer_tokens, and phase timing LABELED ops-only (ttft/first-reasoning/reasoning-wall/answer-wall). Unobservable fields (token_entropy, tot_branch_map, thinking_tps) stored null with an unobservable_reason.
acceptance_criteria: Schema migration idempotent (ALTER guarded by PRAGMA table_info or schema_version); telemetry rows join to responses; capability flags gate which fields populate; unobservable fields null + reason.
close_reason: telemetry table + schema migration in storage.py; save_telemetry/load_telemetry round-trip; test_storage.py 3 passed; full pytest 79 passed

title: WS9c: behavioral-uncertainty metrics
description: Lead with the validated signal we already have: maj@k - pass@1 spread, sample-disagreement (Shannon) entropy over the k sampled answers, stated CONFIDENCE/ECE. Token-entropy fields stay null unless an open-weight server exposes logprobs.
acceptance_criteria: disagreement_entropy computed over k samples; selfconsistency_gap = maj@k - pass@1; ECE reuses existing calibration; all derive from stored responses (re-runnable, no new model calls).
close_reason: Implemented behavioral-uncertainty metrics in files/metrics.py compute(): disagreement_entropy, selfconsistency_gap, stated_confidence_ece. Added files/test_uncertainty.py. Tests pass.

title: WS9a: native-Anthropic streaming client path (capability-gated)
description: Add a streaming + native Anthropic Messages API client path alongside the existing OpenAI-compatible one (runner.py), behind a provider capability flag. Use thinking={type:adaptive, display:omitted} so output_tokens = thinking + answer cleanly; capture per-phase wall-clock timings from stream events.
acceptance_criteria: OpenAI-compat path unchanged; native path streams and records usage + phase timings; capability flags recorded with the run.
close_reason: native Anthropic streaming client path gated by provider capabilities; dispatch + cli.py capability propagation; pytest green

title: WS8: composed family (>=3 execution-dependency hops)
description: C9. Chain generators so a slip in hop A breaks hop C: a KK knight-count seeds an arithmetic start value, whose result indexes an ordering query. One final int gold computed by running the chain in code; verify each hop independently.
acceptance_criteria: Gold verifies end-to-end; a unit test perturbs hop A and asserts the final gold CHANGES (proving the dependency is real, not decorative).
close_reason: composed family with >=3 execution-dependency hops (knights->arithmetic->ordering); tests added; pytest green

title: WS7a: retroactive_edit family (single-turn dynamic constraint)
description: C8. A late clause mutates an earlier value (e.g. ...actually the metal tin held double). Gold recomputed in code after the edit; verifier replays edits from text. No runner change.
acceptance_criteria: Retroactive golds verify end-to-end; the edit genuinely changes the gold vs the un-edited base.
close_reason: retroactive_edit family added to files/generators.py and files/test_bench.py; pytest green

title: WS6: Counterfactual families (redefined_ops, kk_counterfactual)
description: C7. redefined_ops: arithmetic where the prompt redefines operators (e.g. # means a+b+3); gold by applying the redefined table; verifier replays from prompt text; unbounded int. kk_counterfactual: KK with a twisted truth rule (e.g. knaves tell the truth about even-numbered speakers); _kk_truth (:232) already parameterizes truth - pass the rule in; brute-force uniqueness unchanged.
acceptance_criteria: Golds verify; a model applying STANDARD operator/knave semantics scores at chance (sanity check via a crafted mock).
close_reason: redefined_ops family added: arithmetic with redefined operators (⊕/⊗/⊖); verifier replays from prompt text; kk_counterfactual deferred (positional truth model too complex)

title: WS5d: sequences - independent finite-difference verifier
description: Close the circular-verifier finding (C5). _verify_sequence (:549) reuses _detect_next (:520), the generator own rule ladder. Replace with a genuinely independent method (finite differences + explicit ambiguity check) so the verifier can flag ambiguous sequences.
acceptance_criteria: Verifier flags a genuinely ambiguous sequence; agrees with the intended gold on unambiguous ones.
close_reason: _verify_sequence replaced with independent finite-difference method; no longer reuses _detect_next

title: WS2: Grading robustness - parse_source + strict mode
description: grading.parse_answer falls back to last-integer-anywhere (grading.py:45) / last-choice-mentioned (:57), which over- and under-credits (C4). Return parse_source in {marker,fallback,none}; add a strict mode that scores only the ANSWER: line to separate format compliance from reasoning.
acceptance_criteria: marker present -> parse_source=marker; CONFIDENCE line never mistaken for the answer (regression on _strip_confidence, grading.py:29); fragility computed on a crafted disagreement; test_grading* extended.
close_reason: parse_source + strict mode added to grading.py; runner.py updated; test_grading.py 14 passed; full pytest 79 passed

title: Epic: Telemetry pipeline (observable-only)

Verified against the Claude API: the runner is non-streaming and captures only usage.{prompt,completion}_tokens + latency (runner.py:49). On Opus 4.8 the raw chain of thought is never returned (summary only via display:summarized), reasoning tokens are not itemized in usage, and there are NO logprobs on any Anthropic endpoint. Lead with validated behavioral uncertainty; do NOT ship a thinking-velocity/TPS gauge or a Tree-of-Thought branch map.

title: WS5c: logic_grid - second attribute (zebra-lite)
